feedback_items.title: Good
feedback_items.text: Spotify is easily one of the best music streaming platforms out there. The interface is clean and easy to use, and it makes discovering new music feel effortless. I love how personalized the playlists are—especially Discover Weekly and Release Radar, which always seem to match my taste perfectly. The huge library means I can find almost any song or artist I’m looking for, and the ability to create and share playlists is a big plus. The only downside is the ads on the free version, but overall, Spotify offers an amazing listening experience. If you enjoy music and want a platform that helps you explore new sounds while keeping all your favorites in one place, Spotify is definitely worth it.

Most of the genre evolutions died out when Spotify + YouTube became the main way of finding new music imo. Out went radio DJs, magazines, and even websites like Pitchfork (boo) and Stereogum (<3) as tastemakers, in came playlists and algorithmic discovery.

I think - my sample here is my kids & their mates - music discovery is just very different now and the age of what they are listening to matters less. Stuff being used on TikTok, random Spotify playlists, etc rather than the music press or radio play lists. And there’s no role for music TV channels.
